A Redhat Certified Architect and a proud Kubestronaut, Harvinder has demonstrated mastery in Kubernetes, container management, and Redhat solutions. His deep understanding of VMware technologies further complements his ability to design, deploy, and optimize enterprise infrastructure. Passionate about continuous learning and exploring the latest technologies, Harvinder is dedicated to driving efficiency, scalability, and performance for modern enterprises.

person.name: Hieu Hoang
person.bio: Hello everyone, I’m Hieu, with extensive experience in designing and managing cloud-native solutions. I specialize in working across both on-premise and cloud environments, leveraging technologies like Kubernetes and other tools from the CNCF landscape. I’m passionate about automating workflows, optimizing CI/CD pipelines. I also enjoy learning and exploring new technologies to stay updated in the field.

person.name: Pajri Zahrawaani Ahmad
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer (or Debug Engineer maybe) with a strong interest in cloud-native technologies and platform operations. Many CNCF projects that I use at work to solve issues in observability, automation, and improving system reliability in production environments.

person.name: Annie Talvasto
person.bio: Annie Talvasto is an award-winning international technology speaker & marketing leader. She has been recognised with the CNCF Ambassador & Azure MVP awards. Annie has been a co-organizer of Kubernetes & CNCF Finland meetup since 2017 and has spoken at technology conferences across the world, including KubeCon + CloudNativeCon, Microsoft Build & Ignite, KCDC and NDC. Annie also has hosted Cloud Native Live, a weekly livestream by CNCF, since 2021.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I believe in the positive impact open source technology and CNCF projects have in the world, and I am excited to continue spreading the word about them!

person.name: Daryl Ang
person.bio: Support Engineer at Canonical

person.name: Edwin Van De Ven
person.bio: With my background in software development, (Linux) infrastructure, DevOps, Cloud and Cloud Native technologies, I support organizations of various sizes, operating in various fields with their IT needs and wishes. Depending on the project, I work on the technical architecture, guiding & assisting with implementations and making sure solutions are reliable, scalable, secure and maintainable.

person.name: Stefan Kranich
person.bio: I am passionate about building bridges between Dev and Ops. With full commitment, I work to ensure that my team can develop, deploy, operate, and scale software reliably, securely, and cost-effectively.  In my projects, I have repeatedly experienced how much more smoothly software is created when teams take shared responsibility instead of working in silos. I am convinced that the best solutions emerge through genuine collaboration. Promoting and technically supporting this collaboration is what drives me.

person.name: Florian Jäger
person.bio: Hi, I work at an Austrian university, where I specialise in administering Kubernetes clusters and optimising user workloads. Our goal is to provide stable, reproducible research environments and reduce the setup burden for researchers and educators. To achieve this, we leverage CNCF technologies such as Argo, Harbor, and Prometheus, among others.

person.name: Luca Cavallin
person.bio: Senior Software Engineer at GitHub Actions, passionate about developer and infrastructure tools, distributed systems, systems- and network-programming, sharing knowledge. My expertise primarily revolves around Go, Kubernetes, serverless architectures and the Cloud Native domain in general. Off-hours: photography, cycling, kamados, cats.

person.name: Zefeng Yu
person.bio: A Python and Rust coder, specializing in K8S. So far, I am working on DevOps in my company.

person.name: Alessandro Vozza
person.bio: Community leader and CNCF ambassador, Alessandro has spent the last few years building cloud native infrastructures for Microsoft customers, animating the Dutch community, and training others to pass the CKx exams. He has passion for all things cloud native, he's been around open source for 25 years and recently founded a startup that offers innovative Namespaces-as-a-service for developers. Twitter handle: @bongoWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Because I was before and it was awesome!

person.name: Amro Elamin
person.bio: A cloud architect that’s enthusiastic about container orchestration and open source cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Damien Jacinto
person.bio: I am a freelance cloud-native engineer and trainer based in France

person.name: Diptiranjan Muduli
person.bio: I am a tech enthusiast and practitioner specializing in cloud computing, containerization, automation, Infrastructure, DevOps, and Kubernetes. I am passionate about building scalable, secure, and efficient systems, with a strong focus on leveraging Kubernetes and other CNCF technologies to drive automation and innovation. I have a keen interest in cloud-native security, ensuring robust and resilient solutions for modern workloads.

person.name: Jialiang Gao
person.bio: I am a hands-on Cloud & Data/AI Architect in building and delivering complex, scalable cloud-native platforms. I am specialized in Google Cloud, Kubernetes, DevOps, Data Platform and advanced AI technologies. My focus is on architectural design, implementation, and fostering innovation.

person.name: Andres Ortega
person.bio: My name is Andrés Ortega, a Colombian living in Austria. My background is quite different from my current career path: I first earned a bachelor’s degree in Chemistry and Chemical Engineering. Later, I moved to Austria to pursue a master’s degree in Sustainable Energy Systems, which led me to an internship at the Fraunhofer Institute in Kassel. There, I completed my master’s thesis, bridging my earlier academic background with applications in AI.  After graduating, I began working as a data analyst, where I became involved in projects that introduced me to DevOps and MLOps. Driven by curiosity and a hands-on learning approach, I built my own local lab environment to deepen my skills. This journey eventually led me to pursue CNCF certifications, culminating in the achievement of the Kubestronaut badge, a milestone that reflects my passion for cloud-native technologies and continuous learning.  Whenever possible, I actively participate in the CNCF Cloud Native Linz community, sharing knowledge and learning from peers. Through these experiences, I continue to grow my expertise while contributing to the broader cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Hamdi Khelil
person.bio: SRE / DevOps / Cloud Engineer specialized in Kubernetes platform management. Expert in orchestration, automation, and security. Possessing strong skills in creating, deploying, and optimizing clusters. I am passionate about innovation and constantly seeking ways to improve the performance and reliability of container-based infrastructures.

person.name: Nguyễn Ngọc Tiểu Long
person.bio: As a CNCF-certified professional and Kubestronaut, I actively contributes to the community by publishing Helm charts, including the iris-webapp chart on Artifact Hub (https://artifacthub.io/packages/helm/iris-webapp/iris-webapp ),  And by sharing technical insights and hands-on experiences at https://techinsightsworld.com/ I passionate about advancing open-source collaboration and supporting the growth of the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Paolo Patierno
person.bio: I am a Senior Principal Software Engineer working at IBM on messaging and data streaming technologies. One of these is Strimzi, a CNCF project for running Apache Kafka on Kubernetes in a cloud native manner using operators. I am also a committer on a number of IoT related projects and interested in observability and metrics – as a side project I look at how Formula 1 telemetry can be analysed with the technologies I work with every day. I am based in Napoli, where I am the lead organiser of the local CNCF Meetup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I feel that I am one of the lucky people who love their job because it's also their passion. Taking this passion to the community by sharing my knowledge about open source technologies is something which excites me. To be a CNCF Ambassador takes this love to the next level, being even more engaged within the cloud native space.

person.name: Guy Menahem
person.bio: Guy was fortunate enough to experience hands-on the entire evolution of cloud-native computing, from bare-metal servers to VMs and ultimately to Kubernetes. He is passionate about sharing his vast knowledge by writing blogs, speaking at global events, creating video content, and contributing to open-source projects by the CNCF. Guy has been dubbed "The Good Guy" by his peers and members of the community for his persistent desire to help others. It’s no wonder then that his day job at Komodor revolves around helping organizations to adopt K8s and solve the challenges that come with it, while his free time is dedicated to building a local community for Solution Architects and maintaining his educational Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/@the_good_guyWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because it allows me to further my mission of sharing knowledge and driving the adoption of cloud-native technologies. As an ambassador, I will have the opportunity to create more resources, collaboration opportunities, and initiatives with the community & end-users to promote adoption and innovation.

person.name: Nagababu Arepalli
person.bio: Software Engineer working in JPMorgan Chase. As part of BAU learnt Kubernetes and found it interesting and started exploring Kubernetes. Somehow come across Kubestronaut  program and took it as a challenge and achieved it.

person.name: Nagananda J
person.bio: I am Nagananda J, a Senior Software Engineer at Red Hat with over 11 years of experience in telecommunications and cloud computing. I have deep expertise in Kubernetes and container orchestration. My career spans leading organizations such as Ericsson, Cisco, Nokia, and ZTE, where I specialized in 5G Core networks, OpenShift, and cloud infrastructure. I am based in Bengaluru, India.

person.name: Nick Liszewski
person.bio: I’m Nick, an engineer who can’t resist a good puzzle — whether it’s optimizing a pipeline, untangling an infrastructure issue, or tracking down the best coffee in Seattle. My background is in economics, so I tend to approach tech problems with a systems-thinking mindset, and lately I’ve been diving deep into the AI infrastructure space. Outside of work, I’m learning Mandarin and exploring cinema from every corner of the world. Keep building, keep learning, and don’t be afraid to take the unconventional path — it’s where the best stories come from.

person.name: Ahmed Doban
person.bio: I'm a Cloud Technical Lead with extensive experience designing and managing cloud solutions across AWS, GCP, and Oracle Cloud. I specialize in cloud architecture, infrastructure automation, and platform engineering, helping teams build scalable and resilient systems.

person.name: Larry Lai Yanlin
person.bio: Larry Lai is a Cloud & DevOps Engineer based in Singapore, specializing in Kubernetes, GitOps, and secure multi-cloud infrastructure. He is a Kubestronaut recognized by The Linux Foundation, holding certifications including CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCSA, KCNA, PCA, and LFCS, along with multiple AWS, Azure, and OCI credentials. Larry is passionate about CNCF technologies, with hands-on experience in service mesh (Istio), observability (Prometheus, Thanos, Loki), and GitOps automation (ArgoCD, Flux).
